page: Amazon Web Services c8a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores using the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c8a.16xlarge in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read bandwidth of 12,550.52 GB/sec for cached blocks and 412.39 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while Passmark memory performance ranks in the top 25%. The processor's instruction set extensions, including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, drive top-tier results in database operations, static web serving at 109.64 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 193.36 million operations per second.

Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, this instance is designed for CPU-bound workloads that rely on network-attached storage. It is highly efficient for software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing, which all register top-tier benchmark scores. Additionally, the architecture demonstrates strong capabilities for CPU-based machine learning, processing LLM inference prompts at 323.3 tokens per second on medium 7B models. The server is optimal for high-performance computing, batch processing, and distributed analytics where raw compute power per gigabyte of RAM is the primary requirement.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a 30 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core and multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It excels in compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring dense, high-frequency processing power without the overhead of excess memory or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz with a 1:2 core-to-RAM ratio
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
No local storage or GPU hardware, maximizing compute-to-cost efficiency
